comments.message:
unlike you i never said that i have stats that prove you wrong, so i don't understand why you expect any proof from me. my original point was that majority of active players and top players used stable, and you can very easily see that its true just by being active in the community yourself. if you have anything that refutes this claim, please show me it. 

also "Cause it's difficult for me to provide stats to counter your claim if you switch your claim every message lol", what do you even mean by this ? my claim hasn't changed a single time. percentage of top players playing stable is compleatly irrelevant for my claim as long as its more than percentage of top players playing lazer. it doesn't matter if its 99/1 or 40/10, my claim would still be correct in both of those cases

included_comments.message:
You're being quite nitpicky about the stats I provide while providing none yourself and feeling pretty fine with your massive exaggerations don't you think ? :p

As I said, it's an approximation of activity. Old players are unlikely to come back to the game just to play one game in 3 months, most osu players play by phases : they're either playing a lot or they're not playing at all (or transitioning from one phase to the other). So yes it's not perfect but your example is very unrealistic.

Alr 99% was an exaggeration, is 5/100 so 95% also an exaggeration or is it your final word ? :p Cause it's difficult for me to provide stats to counter your claim if you switch your claim every message lol
Give me an actual stat you believe in, what you consider to be top players, and then we can talk.

And I never argued top players were in majority lazer mains btw. My guess from what I've seen is maybe 10-20% main lazer, 40% main stable and the rest are inactive or are switching between the 2, something like that. Maybe you can check if you want instead of playing bad faith simulator.
included_comments.message_html:
You're being quite nitpicky about the stats I provide while providing none yourself and feeling pretty fine with your massive exaggerations don't you think ? :p

As I said, it's an approximation of activity. Old players are unlikely to come back to the game just to play one game in 3 months, most osu players play by phases : they're either playing a lot or they're not playing at all (or transitioning from one phase to the other). So yes it's not perfect but your example is very unrealistic.

Alr 99% was an exaggeration, is 5/100 so 95% also an exaggeration or is it your final word ? :p Cause it's difficult for me to provide stats to counter your claim if you switch your claim every message lol
Give me an actual stat you believe in, what you consider to be top players, and then we can talk.

And I never argued top players were in majority lazer mains btw. My guess from what I've seen is maybe 10-20% main lazer, 40% main stable and the rest are inactive or are switching between the 2, something like that. Maybe you can check if you want instead of playing bad faith simulator.
